name: FIM: Sensitive Secrets File Modified (.env, .pem, .key, credentials)
description: Detects modification of secrets files: .env, credentials, .pem, .key, .pfx. Covers both ECS auditbeat file events and Wazuh FIM (syscheck) alerts forwarded to Elastic. MITRE T1565.001 - Stored Data Manipulation, T1552.001 - Credentials in Files.
query: (event.category:"file" and event.action:("changed-file-content" or "overwrite") and (file.name:(".env" or "*.env") or file.path:("*.pem" or "*.key" or "*.pfx" or "*credentials*" or "*secrets*"))) or (rule.groups:("syscheck" or "syscheck_file") and syscheck.path:("*.env" or "*.pem" or "*.key" or "*.pfx" or "*credentials*" or "*secrets*"))

name: FIM: Secrets File Modified AND Permissions Changed (Attacker Covering Tracks)
description: EQL sequence: secrets file content changed AND permissions changed within 60 seconds on the same host. The permission change after content modification is a strong indicator of an attacker hiding file access. MITRE T1565.001, T1222.002.
query:
sequence by host.name, file.path with maxspan=60s
  [file where event.action in ("changed-file-content", "overwrite") and
    file.name like~ ("*.env", "*.pem", "*.key", "*credentials*", "*secrets*")]
  [file where event.action in ("changed-file-permissions", "chmod")]

subtechnique.name: Linux and Mac File and Directory Permissions Modification

name: FIM: Secrets File Deleted — Possible Exfiltration and Cleanup
description: Detects deletion of secrets/credentials files. Attackers typically exfiltrate credentials then delete the evidence. The deletion itself is an indicator of compromise. MITRE T1070.004 - File Deletion, T1552.001.
query:
file where event.type == "deletion" and
  file.name like~ ("*.env", "*.pem", "*.key", "*.pfx", "*credentials*", "*secrets*", "*.aws/credentials") and
  not process.name in ("shred", "srm", "secure-delete")

name: FIM: SSH authorized_keys Modified — Persistence via Key Injection
description: Detects modification of ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on any host. Attackers inject their SSH public key to establish persistent backdoor access that survives password changes. MITRE T1098.004 - SSH Authorized Keys.
query: (event.category:"file" and event.action:("changed-file-content" or "creation") and file.name:"authorized_keys") or (rule.groups:("syscheck") and syscheck.path:"*authorized_keys*")

name: FIM: Sudoers File Modified — Privilege Escalation via Sudo Rule
description: Detects modification of /etc/sudoers or files under /etc/sudoers.d/. Attackers add 'ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL' entries to gain permanent passwordless root access. MITRE T1548.003 - Sudo and Sudo Caching Abuse.
query: (event.category:"file" and event.action:("changed-file-content" or "creation") and (file.path:"/etc/sudoers" or file.path:"/etc/sudoers.d/*")) or (rule.groups:("syscheck") and syscheck.path:("*sudoers*"))
